NKJV.1: A Psalm of Asaph. The Mighty One, God the LORD, Has spoken and called the earth From the rising of the sun to its going down.
NKJV.2: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty,  God will shine forth.
NKJV.3: Our God shall come, and shall not keep silent;  A fire shall devour before Him, And it shall be very tempestuous all around Him.
NKJV.4: He shall call to the heavens from above, And to the earth, that He may judge His people:
NKJV.5: “Gather My saints together to Me,  Those who have made a covenant with Me by sacrifice.”
NKJV.6: Let the heavens declare His righteousness, For God Himself is Judge. Selah
NKJV.7: “Hear, O My people, and I will speak, O Israel, and I will testify against you; I am God, your God!
NKJV.8: I will not rebuke you for your sacrifices Or your burnt offerings, Which are continually before Me.
NKJV.9: I will not take a bull from your house, Nor goats out of your folds.
NKJV.10: For every beast of the forest is Mine, And the cattle on a thousand hills.
NKJV.11: I know all the birds of the mountains, And the wild beasts of the field are Mine.
NKJV.12: “If I were hungry, I would not tell you; For the world is Mine, and all its fullness.
NKJV.13: Will I eat the flesh of bulls, Or drink the blood of goats?
NKJV.14: Offer to God thanksgiving, And pay your vows to the Most High.
NKJV.15: Call upon Me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you shall glorify Me.”
NKJV.16: But to the wicked God says: “What right have you to declare My statutes, Or take My covenant in your mouth,
NKJV.17: Seeing you hate instruction And cast My words behind you?
NKJV.18: When you saw a thief, you consented with him, And have been a partaker with adulterers.
NKJV.19: You give your mouth to evil, And your tongue frames deceit.
NKJV.20: You sit and speak against your brother; You slander your own mother’s son.
NKJV.21: These things you have done, and I kept silent; You thought that I was altogether like you; But I will rebuke you, And set them in order before your eyes.
NKJV.22: “Now consider this, you who forget God, Lest I tear you in pieces, And there be none to deliver:
NKJV.23: Whoever offers praise glorifies Me; And to him who orders his conduct aright I will show the salvation of God.”

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of Asaph<S>623</S>. The mighty<S>410</S> God<S>430</S>, even the LORD<S>3068</S>, hath spoken<S>1696</S>, and called<S>7121</S> the earth<S>776</S> from the rising<S>4217</S> of the sun<S>8121</S> unto the going down<S>3996</S> thereof. <sup>of: or, for Asaph</sup>
KJV.2: Out of Zion<S>6726</S>, the perfection<S>4359</S> of beauty<S>3308</S>, God<S>430</S> hath shined<S>3313</S>.
KJV.3: Our God<S>430</S> shall come<S>935</S>, and shall not keep silence<S>2790</S>: a fire<S>784</S> shall devour<S>398</S> before<S>6440</S> him, and it shall be very<S>3966</S> tempestuous<S>8175</S> round about<S>5439</S> him.
KJV.4: He shall call<S>7121</S> to the heavens<S>8064</S> from above<S>5920</S>, and to the earth<S>776</S>, that he may judge<S>1777</S> his people<S>5971</S>.
KJV.5: Gather<S>622</S> my saints<S>2623</S> together unto me; those that have made<S>3772</S> a covenant<S>1285</S> with me by sacrifice<S>2077</S>.
KJV.6: And the heavens<S>8064</S> shall declare<S>5046</S> his righteousness<S>6664</S>: for God<S>430</S> is judge<S>8199</S> himself. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.7: Hear<S>8085</S>, O my people<S>5971</S>, and I will speak<S>1696</S>; O Israel<S>3478</S>, and I will testify<S>5749</S> against thee: I am God<S>430</S>, even thy God<S>430</S>.
KJV.8: I will not reprove<S>3198</S> thee for thy sacrifices<S>2077</S> or thy burnt offerings<S>5930</S>, to have been continually<S>8548</S> before me.
KJV.9: I will take<S>3947</S> no bullock<S>6499</S> out of thy house<S>1004</S>, nor he goats<S>6260</S> out of thy folds<S>4356</S>.
KJV.10: For every beast<S>2416</S> of the forest<S>3293</S> is mine, and the cattle<S>929</S> upon a thousand<S>505</S> hills<S>2042</S>.
KJV.11: I know<S>3045</S> all the fowls<S>5775</S> of the mountains<S>2022</S>: and the wild beasts<S>2123</S> of the field<S>7704</S> are mine<S>5978</S>. <sup>mine: Heb. with me</sup>
KJV.12: If I were hungry<S>7456</S>, I would not tell<S>559</S> thee: for the world<S>8398</S> is mine, and the fulness<S>4393</S> thereof.
KJV.13: Will I eat<S>398</S> the flesh<S>1320</S> of bulls<S>47</S>, or drink<S>8354</S> the blood<S>1818</S> of goats<S>6260</S>?
KJV.14: Offer<S>2076</S> unto God<S>430</S> thanksgiving<S>8426</S>; and pay<S>7999</S> thy vows<S>5088</S> unto the most High<S>5945</S>:
KJV.15: And call<S>7121</S> upon me in the day<S>3117</S> of trouble<S>6869</S>: I will deliver<S>2502</S> thee, and thou shalt glorify<S>3513</S> me.
KJV.16: But unto the wicked<S>7563</S> God<S>430</S> saith<S>559</S>, What hast thou to do to declare<S>5608</S> my statutes<S>2706</S>, or that thou shouldest take<S>5375</S> my covenant<S>1285</S> in thy mouth<S>6310</S>?
KJV.17: Seeing thou hatest<S>8130</S> instruction<S>4148</S>, and castest<S>7993</S> my words<S>1697</S> behind<S>310</S> thee.
KJV.18: When thou sawest<S>7200</S> a thief<S>1590</S>, then thou consentedst<S>7521</S> with him, and hast been partaker<S>2506</S> with adulterers<S>5003</S>. <sup>hast: Heb. thy portion was with</sup>
KJV.19: Thou givest<S>7971</S> thy mouth<S>6310</S> to evil<S>7451</S>, and thy tongue<S>3956</S> frameth<S>6775</S> deceit<S>4820</S>. <sup>givest: Heb. sendest</sup>
KJV.20: Thou sittest<S>3427</S> and speakest<S>1696</S> against thy brother<S>251</S>; thou<S>5414</S> slanderest<S>1848</S> thine own mother's<S>517</S> son<S>1121</S>.
KJV.21: These things hast thou done<S>6213</S>, and I kept silence<S>2790</S>; thou thoughtest<S>1819</S> that I was altogether<S>1961</S> such an one as thyself: but I will reprove<S>3198</S> thee, and set them in order<S>6186</S> before thine eyes<S>5869</S>.
KJV.22: Now consider<S>995</S> this, ye that forget<S>7911</S> God<S>433</S>, lest I tear you in pieces<S>2963</S>, and there be none to deliver<S>5337</S>.
KJV.23: Whoso offereth<S>2076</S> praise<S>8426</S> glorifieth<S>3513</S> me: and to him that ordereth<S>7760</S> his conversation<S>1870</S> aright will I shew<S>7200</S> the salvation<S>3468</S> of God<S>430</S>. <sup>that: Heb. that disposeth his way</sup>

ESV.1: The Mighty One, God the LORD, speaks and summons the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.
ESV.2: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines forth.
ESV.3: Our God comes; he does not keep silence;  before him is a devouring fire, around him a mighty tempest.
ESV.4: He calls to the heavens above and to the earth, that he may judge his people:
ESV.5: “Gather to me my faithful ones, who made a covenant with me by sacrifice!”
ESV.6: The heavens declare his righteousness, for God himself is judge! Selah
ESV.7: “Hear, O my people, and I will speak; O Israel, I will testify against you. I am God, your God.
ESV.8: Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you; your burnt offerings are continually before me.
ESV.9: I will not accept a bull from your house or goats from your folds.
ESV.10: For every beast of the forest is mine, the cattle on a thousand hills.
ESV.11: I know all the birds of the hills, and all that moves in the field is mine.
ESV.12: “If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the world and its fullness are mine.
ESV.13: Do I eat the flesh of bulls or drink the blood of goats?
ESV.14: Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving,  and perform your vows to the Most High,
ESV.15: and call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you shall glorify me.”
ESV.16: But to the wicked God says: “What right have you to recite my statutes or take my covenant on your lips?
ESV.17: For you hate discipline, and you cast my words behind you.
ESV.18: If you see a thief, you are pleased with him, and you keep company with adulterers.
ESV.19: “You give your mouth free rein for evil, and your tongue frames deceit.
ESV.20: You sit and speak against your brother; you slander your own mother's son.
ESV.21: These things you have done, and I have been silent; you thought that I  was one like yourself. But now I rebuke you and lay the charge before you.
ESV.22: “Mark this, then, you who forget God, lest I tear you apart, and there be none to deliver!
ESV.23: The one who offers thanksgiving as his sacrifice glorifies me; to one who orders his way rightly I will show the salvation of God!”

NASB.1: A Psalm of Asaph. The Mighty One, God, the LORD, has spoken, And summoned the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.
NASB.2: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God has shone forth.
NASB.3: May our God come and not keep silence; Fire devours before Him, And it is very tempestuous around Him.
NASB.4: He summons the heavens above, And the earth, to judge His people:
NASB.5: 'Gather My godly ones to Me, Those who have made a covenant with Me by sacrifice.'
NASB.6: And the heavens declare His righteousness, For God Himself is judge. Selah.
NASB.7: 'Hear, O My people, and I will speak; O Israel, I will testify against you; I am God, your God.
NASB.8: 'I do not reprove you for your sacrifices, And your burnt offerings are continually before Me.
NASB.9: 'I shall take no young bull out of your house Nor male goats out of your folds.
NASB.10: 'For every beast of the forest is Mine, The cattle on a thousand hills.
NASB.11: 'I know every bird of the mountains, And everything that moves in the field is Mine.
NASB.12: 'If I were hungry I would not tell you, For the world is Mine, and all it contains.
NASB.13: 'Shall I eat the flesh of bulls Or drink the blood of male goats?
NASB.14: 'Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving And pay your vows to the Most High;
NASB.15: Call upon Me in the day of trouble; I shall rescue you, and you will honor Me.'
NASB.16: But to the wicked God says, 'What right have you to tell of My statutes And to take My covenant in your mouth?
NASB.17: 'For you hate discipline, And you cast My words behind you.
NASB.18: 'When you see a thief, you are pleased with him, And you associate with adulterers.
NASB.19: 'You let your mouth loose in evil And your tongue frames deceit.
NASB.20: 'You sit and speak against your brother; You slander your own mother's son.
NASB.21: 'These things you have done and I kept silence; You thought that I was just like you; I will reprove you and state [the case] in order before your eyes.
NASB.22: 'Now consider this, you who forget God, Or I will tear [you] in pieces, and there will be none to deliver.
NASB.23: 'He who offers a sacrifice of thanksgiving honors Me; And to him who orders [his] way [aright] I shall show the salvation of God.'

NLT.1:
The LORD, the Mighty One, is God,
and he has spoken;
he has summoned all humanity
from where the sun rises to where it sets.
NLT.2:
From Mount Zion, the perfection of beauty,
God shines in glorious radiance.
NLT.3:
Our God approaches,
and he is not silent.
Fire devours everything in his way,
and a great storm rages around him.
NLT.4:
He calls on the heavens above and earth below
to witness the judgment of his people.
NLT.5:
“Bring my faithful people to me —
those who made a covenant with me by giving sacrifices.”
NLT.6:
Then let the heavens proclaim his justice,
for God himself will be the judge. Interlude
NLT.7:
“O my people, listen as I speak.
Here are my charges against you, O Israel:
I am God, your God!
NLT.8:
I have no complaint about your sacrifices
or the burnt offerings you constantly offer.
NLT.9:
But I do not need the bulls from your barns
or the goats from your pens.
NLT.10:
For all the animals of the forest are mine,
and I own the cattle on a thousand hills.
NLT.11:
I know every bird on the mountains,
and all the animals of the field are mine.
NLT.12:
If I were hungry, I would not tell you,
for all the world is mine and everything in it.
NLT.13:
Do I eat the meat of bulls?
Do I drink the blood of goats?
NLT.14:
Make thankfulness your sacrifice to God,
and keep the vows you made to the Most High.
NLT.15:
Then call on me when you are in trouble,
and I will rescue you,
and you will give me glory.”
NLT.16:
But God says to the wicked:
“Why bother reciting my decrees
and pretending to obey my covenant?
NLT.17:
For you refuse my discipline
and treat my words like trash.
NLT.18:
When you see thieves, you approve of them,
and you spend your time with adulterers.
NLT.19:
Your mouth is filled with wickedness,
and your tongue is full of lies.
NLT.20:
You sit around and slander your brother —
your own mother’s son.
NLT.21:
While you did all this, I remained silent,
and you thought I didn’t care.
But now I will rebuke you,
listing all my charges against you.
NLT.22:
Repent, all of you who forget me,
or I will tear you apart,
and no one will help you.
NLT.23:
But giving thanks is a sacrifice that truly honors me.
If you keep to my path,
I will reveal to you the salvation of God.”

WEB.1: A Psalm by Asaph. The Mighty One, God, Yahweh, speaks, and calls the earth from sunrise to sunset.
WEB.2: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines out.
WEB.3: Our God comes, and does not keep silent. A fire devours before him. It is very stormy around him.
WEB.4: He calls to the heavens above, to the earth, that he may judge his people:
WEB.5: “Gather my saints together to me, those who have made a covenant with me by sacrifice.”
WEB.6: The heavens shall declare his righteousness, for God himself is judge. Selah.
WEB.7: “Hear, my people, and I will speak; Israel, and I will testify against you. I am God, your God.
WEB.8: I don’t rebuke you for your sacrifices. Your burnt offerings are continually before me.
WEB.9: I have no need for a bull from your stall, nor male goats from your pens.
WEB.10: For every animal of the forest is mine, and the livestock on a thousand hills.
WEB.11: I know all the birds of the mountains. The wild animals of the field are mine.
WEB.12: If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the world is mine, and all that is in it.
WEB.13: Will I eat the meat of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?
WEB.14: Offer to God the sacrifice of thanksgiving. Pay your vows to the Most High.
WEB.15: Call on me in the day of trouble. I will deliver you, and you will honor me.”
WEB.16: But to the wicked God says, “What right do you have to declare my statutes, that you have taken my covenant on your lips,
WEB.17: since you hate instruction, and throw my words behind you?
WEB.18: When you saw a thief, you consented with him, and have participated with adulterers.
WEB.19: “You give your mouth to evil. Your tongue frames deceit.
WEB.20: You sit and speak against your brother. You slander your own mother’s son.
WEB.21: You have done these things, and I kept silent. You thought that I was just like you. I will rebuke you, and accuse you in front of your eyes.
WEB.22: “Now consider this, you who forget God, lest I tear you into pieces, and there be no one to deliver.
WEB.23: Whoever offers the sacrifice of thanksgiving glorifies me, and prepares his way so that I will show God’s salvation to him.”

CSB17.8:

for every animal of the forest is mine,

CSB17.11:
I know every bird of the mountains,
and the creatures of the field are mine. ⓛ

If I were hungry, I would not tell you,

CSB17.13:
Do I eat the flesh of bulls
or drink the blood of goats? ⓝ
CSB17.14:
Sacrifice a thank offering to God, ⓐ
and pay your vows to the Most High. ⓑ
CSB17.15:
Call on me in a day of trouble;
I will rescue you, and you will honor me.” ⓒ
CSB17.16:
But God says to the wicked:
“What right do you have to recite my statutes
and to take my covenant on your lips? ⓓ
CSB17.17:
You hate instruction
and fling my words behind you. ⓔ
CSB17.18:
When you see a thief,
you make friends with him,
and you associate with adulterers. ⓕ
CSB17.19:
You unleash your mouth for evil
and harness your tongue for deceit. ⓖ
CSB17.20:

slandering your mother’s son. ⓗ

you thought I was just like you. ⓘ
But I will rebuke you

Whoever sacrifices a thank offering honors me,

AMP.1:
A Psalm of Asaph The mighty One, God, the LORD, has spoken,
And summoned the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting [from east to west].
AMP.2:
Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty,
God has shone forth.
AMP.3:
May our God come and not keep silent;
Fire devours before Him,
And around Him a mighty tempest rages.
AMP.4:
He summons the heavens above,
And the earth, to judge His people:
AMP.5:
"Gather My godly ones to Me,
Those who have made a covenant with Me by sacrifice."
AMP.6:
And the heavens declare His righteousness,
For God Himself is judge. Selah.
AMP.7:
"Hear, O My people, and I will speak;
O Israel, I will testify against you:
I am God, your God.
AMP.8:
"I do not reprove you for your sacrifices;
Your burnt offerings are continually before Me.
AMP.9:
"I will accept no young bull from your house
Nor male goat from your folds.
AMP.10:
"For every beast of the forest is Mine,
And the cattle on a thousand hills.
AMP.11:
"I know every bird of the mountains,
And everything that moves in the field is Mine.
AMP.12:
"If I were hungry, I would not tell you,
For the world and all it contains are Mine.
AMP.13:
"Shall I eat the flesh of bulls
Or drink the blood of male goats?
AMP.14:
"Offer to God the sacrifice of thanksgiving
And pay your vows to the Most High;
AMP.15:
Call on Me in the day of trouble;
I will rescue you, and you shall honor and glorify Me."
AMP.16:
But to the wicked God says:
"What right have you to recite My statutes
Or to take My covenant on your lips?
AMP.17:
"For you hate instruction and discipline
And cast My words behind you [discarding them].
AMP.18:
"When you see a thief, you are pleased with him and condone his behavior,
And you associate with adulterers.
AMP.19:
"You give your mouth to evil
And your tongue frames deceit.
AMP.20:
"You sit and speak against your brother;
You slander your own mother's son.
AMP.21:
"These things you have done and I kept silent;
You thought that I was just like you.
Now I will reprimand and denounce you and state the case in order before your eyes.
AMP.22:
"Now consider this, you who forget God,
Or I will tear you in pieces, and there will be no one to rescue [you].
AMP.23:
"He who offers a sacrifice of praise and thanksgiving honors Me;
And to him who orders his way rightly [who follows the way that I show him],
I shall show the salvation of God."

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
The God of gods—it's God!—speaks out, shouts, "Earth!"
welcomes the sun in the east,
farewells the disappearing sun in the west.
MSG.2:
From the dazzle of Zion,
God blazes into view.
MSG.3:
Our God makes his entrance,
he's not shy in his coming.
Starbursts of fireworks precede him.
MSG.4:
He summons heaven and earth as a jury,
he's taking his people to court:
MSG.5:
"Round up my saints who swore
on the Bible their loyalty to me."
MSG.6:
The whole cosmos attests to the fairness of this court,
that here God is judge.
MSG.7:
"Are you listening, dear people? I'm getting ready to speak;
Israel, I'm about ready to bring you to trial.
This is God, your God,
speaking to you.
MSG.8:
I don't find fault with your acts of worship,
the frequent burnt sacrifices you offer.
MSG.9:
But why should I want your blue-ribbon bull,
or more and more goats from your herds?
MSG.10:
Every creature in the forest is mine,
the wild animals on all the mountains.
MSG.11:
I know every mountain bird by name;
the scampering field mice are my friends.
MSG.12:
If I get hungry, do you think I'd tell you?
All creation and its bounty are mine.
MSG.13:
Do you think I feast on venison?
or drink draughts of goats' blood?
MSG.14:
Spread for me a banquet of praise,
serve High God a feast of kept promises,
MSG.15:
And call for help when you're in trouble—
I'll help you, and you'll honor me."
MSG.16:
Next, God calls up the wicked:
"What are you up to, quoting my laws,
talking like we are good friends?
MSG.17:
You never answer the door when I call;
you treat my words like garbage.
MSG.18:
If you find a thief, you make him your buddy;
adulterers are your friends of choice.
MSG.19:
Your mouth drools filth;
lying is a serious art form with you.
MSG.20:
You stab your own brother in the back,
rip off your little sister.
MSG.21:
I kept a quiet patience while you did these things;
you thought I went along with your game.
I'm calling you on the carpet, now,
laying your wickedness out in plain sight.
MSG.22:
"Time's up for playing fast and
loose with me.
I'm ready to pass sentence,
and there's no help in sight!
MSG.23:
It's the praising life that honors me.
As soon as you set your foot on the Way,
I'll show you my salvation."

NIV.1:
Psalm 50
A psalm of Asaph.
The Mighty One, God, the Lord,
speaks and summons the earth
from the rising of the sun to the place where it sets.
NIV.2:
From Zion, perfect in beauty,
God shines forth.
NIV.3:
Our God comes and will not be silent;
a fire devours before him,
and around him a tempest rages.
NIV.4:
He summons the heavens above,
and the earth, that he may judge his people:
NIV.5:
“Gather to me my consecrated ones,
who made a covenant with me by sacrifice.”
NIV.6:
And the heavens proclaim his righteousness,
for God himself is judge. Selah
NIV.7:
“Hear, O my people, and I will speak,
O Israel, and I will testify against you:
I am God, your God.
NIV.8:
I do not rebuke you for your sacrifices
or your burnt offerings, which are ever before me.
NIV.9:
I have no need of a bull from your stall
or of goats from your pens,
NIV.10:
for every animal of the forest is mine,
and the cattle on a thousand hills.
NIV.11:
I know every bird in the mountains,
and the creatures of the field are mine.
NIV.12:
If I were hungry I would not tell you,
for the world is mine, and all that is in it.
NIV.13:
Do I eat the flesh of bulls
or drink the blood of goats?
NIV.14:
Sacrifice thank offerings to God,
fulfill your vows to the Most High,
NIV.15:
and call upon me in the day of trouble;
I will deliver you, and you will honor me.”
NIV.16:
But to the wicked, God says:
“What right have you to recite my laws
or take my covenant on your lips?
NIV.17:
You hate my instruction
and cast my words behind you.
NIV.18:
When you see a thief, you join with him;
you throw in your lot with adulterers.
NIV.19:
You use your mouth for evil
and harness your tongue to deceit.
NIV.20:
You speak continually against your brother
and slander your own mother’s son.
NIV.21:
These things you have done and I kept silent;
you thought I was altogether like you.
But I will rebuke you
and accuse you to your face.
NIV.22:
“Consider this, you who forget God,
or I will tear you to pieces, with none to rescue:
NIV.23:
He who sacrifices thank offerings honors me,
and he prepares the way
so that I may show him the salvation of God.”

NRSVCE.1:
The mighty one, God the LORD,
speaks and summons the earth
from the rising of the sun to its setting.
NRSVCE.2:
Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty,
God shines forth.
NRSVCE.3:
Our God comes and does not keep silence,
before him is a devouring fire,
and a mighty tempest all around him.
NRSVCE.4:
He calls to the heavens above
and to the earth, that he may judge his people:
NRSVCE.5:
“Gather to me my faithful ones,
who made a covenant with me by sacrifice!”
NRSVCE.6:
The heavens declare his righteousness,
for God himself is judge. Selah
NRSVCE.7:
“Hear, O my people, and I will speak,
O Israel, I will testify against you.
I am God, your God.
NRSVCE.8:
Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you;
your burnt offerings are continually before me.
NRSVCE.9:
I will not accept a bull from your house,
or goats from your folds.
NRSVCE.10:
For every wild animal of the forest is mine,
the cattle on a thousand hills.
NRSVCE.11:
I know all the birds of the air,
and all that moves in the field is mine.
NRSVCE.12:
“If I were hungry, I would not tell you,
for the world and all that is in it is mine.
NRSVCE.13:
Do I eat the flesh of bulls,
or drink the blood of goats?
NRSVCE.14:
Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving,
and pay your vows to the Most High.
NRSVCE.15:
Call on me in the day of trouble;
I will deliver you, and you shall glorify me.”
NRSVCE.16:
But to the wicked God says:
“What right have you to recite my statutes,
or take my covenant on your lips?
NRSVCE.17:
For you hate discipline,
and you cast my words behind you.
NRSVCE.18:
You make friends with a thief when you see one,
and you keep company with adulterers.
NRSVCE.19:
“You give your mouth free rein for evil,
and your tongue frames deceit.
NRSVCE.20:
You sit and speak against your kin;
you slander your own mother’s child.
NRSVCE.21:
These things you have done and I have been silent;
you thought that I was one just like yourself.
But now I rebuke you, and lay the charge before you.
NRSVCE.22:
“Mark this, then, you who forget God,
or I will tear you apart, and there will be no one to deliver.
NRSVCE.23:
Those who bring thanksgiving as their sacrifice honor me;
to those who go the right way
I will show the salvation of God.”

NET.1: El, God, the LORD speaks, and summons the earth to come from the east and west.
NET.2: From Zion, the most beautiful of all places, God comes in splendor.
NET.3: Our God approaches and is not silent; consuming fire goes ahead of him and all around him a storm rages.
NET.4: He summons the heavens above, as well as the earth, so that he might judge his people.
NET.5: He says: “Assemble my covenant people before me, those who ratified a covenant with me by sacrifice!”
NET.6: The heavens declare his fairness, for God is judge. (Selah)
NET.7: He says: “Listen my people! I am speaking! Listen Israel! I am accusing you! I am God, your God!
NET.8: I am not condemning you because of your sacrifices, or because of your burnt sacrifices that you continually offer me.
NET.9: I do not need to take a bull from your household or goats from your sheepfolds.
NET.10: For every wild animal in the forest belongs to me, as well as the cattle that graze on a thousand hills.
NET.11: I keep track of every bird in the hills, and the insects of the field are mine.
NET.12: Even if I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the world and all it contains belong to me.
NET.13: Do I eat the flesh of bulls? Do I drink the blood of goats?
NET.14: Present to God a thank-offering! Repay your vows to the sovereign One!
NET.15: Pray to me when you are in trouble! I will deliver you, and you will honor me!”
NET.16: God says this to the evildoer: “How can you declare my commands, and talk about my covenant?
NET.17: For you hate instruction and reject my words.
NET.18: When you see a thief, you join him; you associate with men who are unfaithful to their wives.
NET.19: You do damage with words, and use your tongue to deceive.
NET.20: You plot against your brother; you slander your own brother.
NET.21: When you did these things, I was silent, so you thought I was exactly like you. But now I will condemn you and state my case against you!
NET.22: Carefully consider this, you who reject God! Otherwise I will rip you to shreds and no one will be able to rescue you.
NET.23: Whoever presents a thank-offering honors me. To whoever obeys my commands, I will reveal my power to deliver.”
